opening_phrase: This is cyber police, your name is in an investigation
pressure_tactic: Fake law-enforcement authority and threat of arrest
ask: Stay on video call and do not contact anyone
representative_excerpt: Caller claims police or CBI need a video statement and says disconnection will worsen the case.
notes: Use for generic digital-arrest scam variants.

opening_phrase: A narcotics case has been registered using your ID
pressure_tactic: Fear of drug case and criminal record
ask: Move to video call and share identity details
representative_excerpt: Caller poses as anti-narcotics staff and says the citizen is linked to a parcel or drug transaction.
notes: Do not include operational legal document images.

opening_phrase: RBI verification is required to clear your bank accounts
ask: Transfer funds for verification or security deposit
representative_excerpt: Scammer says money must be moved to a safe account and will be returned after verification.
notes: Good contrast against genuine RBI/public-safety notices.

opening_phrase: You are under digital arrest until officers finish checking
ask: Remain visible on camera for hours
representative_excerpt: Victim is told to stay on camera and avoid family, bank staff, or police while scammers supervise transfers.
notes: Useful for help-seeking rows where victim asks if this is legal.

opening_phrase: An arrest warrant has been issued against you
ask: Pay to pause or settle fabricated proceedings
representative_excerpt: Scammer sends a fake warrant or official seal and demands quick payment to stop arrest.
notes: Use paraphrases only; no fake warrant reproduction.

opening_phrase: No agency conducts investigation over phone or video call
ask: Report to 1930 or cybercrime.gov.in
representative_excerpt: Legitimate awareness message tells citizens to stop, think, and report instead of obeying a video-call threat.
notes: Anchor for genuine/help-seeking contrast.

opening_phrase: Approve request to receive your refund
ask: Enter UPI PIN on collect request
representative_excerpt: Fraudster says money will arrive only after the user approves a request, but approval sends money out.
notes: Ground in BHIM approve/decline request flow.

opening_phrase: Scan this QR to get paid
ask: Scan QR or approve request
representative_excerpt: Scammer asks a seller to scan a QR or approve a request to receive money, reversing the payment direction.
notes: HDFC notes QR is for sending, not receiving.

opening_phrase: Your refund has been processed, accept it in UPI
ask: Approve collect request using UPI PIN
representative_excerpt: Caller offers a refund and sends a pull request that requires the victim to authorize payment.
notes: Good for Hinglish and English scam variants.

opening_phrase: Payment confirmation pending from your side
ask: Tap approve and enter UPI PIN
representative_excerpt: Scammer posing as merchant sends a payment request disguised as final confirmation.
notes: Use with official definition of collect request.

opening_phrase: Check verified merchant before approving
ask: Approve only known IPO mandate details
representative_excerpt: Genuine IPO collect requests should show verified merchant and match bid details before approval.
notes: Anchor for genuine UPI collect rows.

opening_phrase: P2P collect requests are being phased out
ask: Do not approve unknown P2P collect requests
representative_excerpt: NPCI circular listing supports risk framing around P2P collect misuse and discontinuation.
notes: Use as metadata source, not as SMS copy.

opening_phrase: Press 9 to speak about your FedEx parcel
ask: Press a key and continue to fake officer
representative_excerpt: Automated call says a parcel linked to the citizen was intercepted and routes them to a fake officer.
notes: Keep carrier name as documented source context.

opening_phrase: A parcel in your name contains MDMA and passports
ask: Share Aadhaar/PAN and join video inquiry
representative_excerpt: Caller alleges illegal items in a parcel and says customs or police have registered a case.
notes: Representative excerpt paraphrased.

opening_phrase: Your Aadhaar was used to send a parcel abroad
ask: Confirm ID details and follow transfer instructions
representative_excerpt: Scammer claims Aadhaar was used for a parcel to Iran or Taiwan and escalates to fake police.
notes: Common in parcel-to-digital-arrest variants.

opening_phrase: I am transferring this call to Mumbai Cyber Crime
ask: Install or open video-call app
representative_excerpt: Courier caller performs a fake call transfer to someone posing as a cybercrime officer.
notes: Useful for surface format transitions.

opening_phrase: Deposit funds with RBI for verification
ask: RTGS/IMPS transfer to so-called safe account
representative_excerpt: Victim is told a refundable verification deposit is needed to clear courier-linked money laundering claims.
notes: Use only as pattern, not as legal advice.

opening_phrase: We do not ask personal information in unsolicited calls
ask: Report suspicious calls and do not share personal info
representative_excerpt: Carrier guidance says unsolicited calls or messages asking for personal info should not be trusted.
notes: Can anchor genuine courier-warning rows.

opening_phrase: Update KYC now or your account will be blocked
ask: Click link and enter login/OTP
representative_excerpt: SMS threatens account freeze unless the user opens a link and submits banking details.
notes: RBI confirms false urgency and account blocking language.

opening_phrase: I am calling from your bank for pending KYC
ask: Share OTP/CVV/PIN or profile password
representative_excerpt: Caller claims KYC is pending and asks for confidential credentials or OTP.
notes: SBI page has KYC/vishing examples.

opening_phrase: Install this support app to complete KYC
ask: Install AnyDesk/TeamViewer style app
representative_excerpt: Fraudster uses KYC update as pretext to make victim install a remote-access or screen-sharing app.
notes: Also covered on RBI/SBI safety guidance.

opening_phrase: PAN update required for your bank KYC
ask: Open link and submit PAN/bank details
representative_excerpt: Message asks the user to update PAN or KYC through a link that captures credentials.
notes: Older but useful for high-frequency fake KYC pattern.

opening_phrase: Share Aadhaar OTP to finish verification
ask: Share Aadhaar OTP or documents
representative_excerpt: Caller or SMS asks for Aadhaar OTP or copies under KYC pretext.
notes: Use to contrast with genuine VID/Aadhaar safety advice.

opening_phrase: Urgent security update for online banking
ask: Click link and enter credentials
representative_excerpt: Phishing message uses bank-like language, urgent subject, and a fake link to capture credentials.
notes: ICICI page lists subject styles and warning signs.

opening_phrase: Read this secrecy affidavit on camera
pressure_tactic: Isolation ritual and fake legal procedure
ask: Take oath, stay silent, and keep video call active
representative_excerpt: Fraudsters force victims to read or sign fake secrecy documents so they do not ask family or police for help.
notes: Useful for help-seeking rows about secrecy oath.

opening_phrase: Supreme Court and Ministry letters are issued in your name
ask: Pay or mortgage assets to continue investigation
representative_excerpt: Scammers send fake court or ministry letters and pressure victims to liquidate savings or take loans.
notes: Good for senior-citizen digital-arrest variant.

opening_phrase: Debit reversal pending, approve request now
ask: Approve collect request and enter UPI PIN
representative_excerpt: Fraudster sends a collect request while claiming it is a debit reversal or refund confirmation.
notes: HDFC notes collect-request fraud for debit reversal/refunds.

opening_phrase: Do not respond to any unknown UPI requests
ask: Decline unknown request and report suspicious request
representative_excerpt: Genuine bank guidance says UPI PIN is needed for transferring money, not receiving money, and unknown UPI requests should not be accepted.
notes: Counterpattern for genuine/help-seeking rows.

opening_phrase: Your courier address has an issue, pay small fee
ask: Install remote app or pay Rs. 2 processing fee
representative_excerpt: Victim calls a fake courier-care number; caller asks for a tiny fee or app install and drains account.
notes: Common non-narcotics courier support scam.

opening_phrase: Delivery agent cannot verify your number, dial this code
ask: Dial USSD call-forwarding code
representative_excerpt: Fake delivery caller asks user to dial a USSD code that forwards calls and OTPs to the attacker.
notes: Use carefully; avoid including real USSD codes in generated scam rows.

opening_phrase: A bank employee will ask for the URN you receive
ask: Share URN/OTP used to add payee
representative_excerpt: Fraudster adds a payee and convinces user to share the URN/OTP needed to authorize it.
notes: ICICI scenario-based phishing education.

opening_phrase: Install security APK to update your KYC
ask: Install third-party APK and grant permissions
representative_excerpt: Message says a bank service or KYC update needs an APK; installed malware can access device data and OTPs.
notes: Avoid real APK links in dataset rows.
